affected_surfaces.note: On-disk path changes from directory to file; existing users need one-time --reset. Error-message path string corrected.
Exit 1 reserved for genuine failures (provider unreachable, store init failed, etc.). Skipped-files-with-zero-chunks is exit 0 with a warning.
Filters dirs before read; Python chunker confirmed/extended to emit chunks for typical class/function patterns.

risks.description: Phase 2's storage-path change could orphan existing user vector stores at .coderef/rag-vectors.sqlite/ (the directory).
risks.mitigation: Detect the legacy directory layout and clean it on --reset. Document in closeout commit message.

risks.description: Phase 3's exit-code change could break CI consumers that gate on rag-index exit code.
risks.mitigation: Document in docs/CLI.md. The change is semver-meaningful but the right semantics; previous behavior was effectively 'always assume failure' for many legitimate runs.

risks.description: Phase 4's chunker fix may produce a much larger index for projects that were partially-skipped before. Disk + indexing time increases.
risks.mitigation: Document expected indexing-time uplift in P4-LLOYD-RESULT.md after re-indexing LLOYD.

test_strategy: Phase 1: reproduce all 3 bugs against a controlled fixture + LLOYD.
Phase 2: write/reset/re-write cycle with dimension change.
Phase 3: 4-scenario exit-code matrix (provider unreachable / no files / all-skipped / partial-success).
Phase 4: chunks_indexed > 0 on LLOYD .py files; per-file skip reasons logged.
Phase 5: full coderef-pipeline 4/4 legs OK on LLOYD.
commit_strategy: One commit per phase (5 total). Close commit optional per /close-workorder canonical flow.
